title: Sprinkler
abstract: A strip irrigator sprinkler (10) has a central axis (X) and includes a stationary body (12) and a diffuser (14) coupled to the stationary body. The diffuser (14) can rotate about the central axis (X) relative to the stationary body (12) and the sprinkler (10) includes in addition at least one outlet opening (122) out of the stationary body (12) that is arranged to emit liquid towards the diffuser (14) along an axis (Y) that extends generally alongside axis (X) at a given lateral side of axis (X).
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A strip irrigator sprinkler having a central axis X and comprising a stationary body and a diffuser coupled to the stationary body and arranged to rotate about the central axis X relative to the stationary body, the sprinkler comprising in addition at least one outlet opening out of the stationary body that is arranged to emit liquid towards the diffuser along an axis Y that extends generally alongside axis X at a given lateral side of axis X. 2. The strip irrigator sprinkler of claim 1 , wherein liquid emitted from the at least one outlet is arranged to impinge against the diffuser to urge it to rotate. 3. The strip irrigator sprinkler of claim 1 and 2, wherein liquid emitted towards the diffuser and engaging the diffuser is arranged to be deflected sideways away from axis X generally in a lateral direction of the given lateral side. 4. The strip irrigator sprinkler of claim 3, wherein interaction between emitted liquid from the at least one outlet and the revolving diffuser is arranged to form a generally rectangular strip of irrigation extending in the direction of the given lateral side. 5. The strip irrigator sprinkler of claim 4, wherein the diffuser comprising a plurality of flow channels formed at its lower side that faces the stationary body. 6. The strip irrigator sprinkler of claim 5, wherein the generally rectangular strip of irrigation is formed by interaction of the emitted liquid with a group of the flow channels. 7. The strip irrigator sprinkler of claim 6, wherein interaction of emitted liquid with each flow channel of the group forms a sub-pattern of irrigation and 

superimposing the sub-patterns of the group one over the other forms the resulting generally rectangular strip of irrigation. 8. The strip irrigator sprinkler of claim 7, wherein at least some of the sub patterns of the group are substantially different in area size than other sub-patterns of the group. 9. The strip irrigator sprinkler of claim 8, wherein a given sub-pattern of the group that is substantially larger in area size than a certain other sub-pattern of the group is arranged to receive more liquid than that deflected to form the certain other smaller sub-pattern. 10. The strip irrigator sprinkler of claim 9, wherein deflecting more liquid to form the given sub-pattern is obtained by directing more than one flow channel of the diffuser to deflect liquid to form the given sub-pattern. 11. The strip irrigator sprinkler of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one outlet is two or more outlets each forming a respective generally rectangular strip of irrigation. 12. The strip irrigator sprinkler of any one of the preceding claims, wherein coupling the diffuser to the stationary body is by snap fitting the diffuser onto a stem of the stationary body. 13. A method of sprinkling liquid for irrigation comprising the steps of: providing a strip irrigator sprinkler having a central axis X and comprising a stationary body and a diffuser coupled to the stationary body, wherein the sprinkler comprising in addition at least one outlet opening out of the stationary body, 

 coupling the strip irrigator sprinkler to an upstream pressurized liquid source and emitting liquid out of the at least one outlet towards the diffuser along an axis Y that extends generally alongside axis X at a given lateral side of axis X, wherein liquid from the at least one outlet impinging against the diffuser is arranged to rotate the diffuser about the central axis X relative to the stationary body and deflect sideways away from axis X generally in a lateral direction of the given lateral side. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein interaction between emitted liquid from the at least one outlet and the revolving diffuser is arranged to form a generally rectangular strip of irrigation extending in the direction of the given lateral side. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the diffuser comprising a plurality of flow channels formed at its lower side that faces the stationary body. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein the generally rectangular strip of irrigation is formed by interaction of the emitted liquid with a group of the flow channels. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein interaction of emitted liquid with each flow channel of the group forms a sub-pattern of irrigation and superimposing the sub-patterns of the group one over the other forms the resulting generally rectangular strip of irrigation. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein at least some of the sub-patterns of the group are substantially different in area size than other sub-patterns of the group. 19. The method of claim 18, wherein a given sub-pattern of the group that is substantially larger in area size than a certain other sub-pattern of the group is arranged to receive more liquid than that deflected to form the certain other smaller sub-pattern. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein deflecting more liquid to form the given sub-pattern is obtained by directing more than one flow channel of the diffuser to deflect liquid to form the given sub-pattern. 21. The method of any one of claims 13 to 20, wherein the at least one outlet is two or more outlets each forming a respective generally rectangular strip of irrigation. 22. A strip irrigator sprinkler having a central axis X and comprising a stationary body and a diffuser coupled to the stationary body and arranged to rotate about the central axis X relative to the stationary body, the sprinkler comprising in addition at least one outlet opening out of the stationary body that is arranged to emit liquid to interact with flow channels formed in the diffuser along an axis Y that extends generally alongside axis X at a given lateral side of axis X, wherein said interaction is arranged to form a generally rectangular strip of irrigation that extends in the direction of the given lateral side that is formed by interaction of the emitted liquid with a group of the flow channels, and wherein emitted liquid interacting with each flow channel of the group forms a sub pattern of irrigation and superimposing the sub-patterns of the group one over the other forms the resulting generally rectangular strip of irrigation.
